section_name: Vehicles Transporting Certain Agricultural Products or Processing Equipment
chapter_title: Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text:
(a) A single motor vehicle used exclusively to transport chile pepper modules, seed cotton, cotton, cotton burrs, or equipment used to transport or process chile pepper modules or cotton, including a motor vehicle or burr spreader, may not be operated on a highway or road if the vehicle is:

(1) wider than 10 feet and the highway has not been designated by the commission under Section 621.202 (Commission’s Authority to Set Maximum Width) ;

(2) longer than 48 feet; or

(3) higher than 14 feet 6 inches.

(b) A single motor vehicle that transports agricultural products under Subsection (a) must be registered under Section 504.505 (Cotton Vehicles) .

(c) A truck-tractor operated in combination with a semitrailer and used to transport seed cotton or cotton may not be operated on a highway or road if the vehicle is higher than 14 feet 6 inches.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 622. Spec. Provisions & Exceptions for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 622.101 — Vehicles Transporting Certain Agricultural Products or Processing Equipment

(a) A single motor vehicle used exclusively to transport chile pepper modules, seed cotton, cotton, cotton burrs, or equipment used to transport or process chile pepper modules or cotton, including a motor vehicle or burr spreader, may not be operated on a highway or road if the vehicle is:

    (1) wider than 10 feet and the highway has not been designated by the commission under Section 621.202 (Commission’s Authority to Set Maximum Width) ;

    (2) longer than 48 feet; or

    (3) higher than 14 feet 6 inches.

(b) A single motor vehicle that transports agricultural products under Subsection (a) must be registered under Section 504.505 (Cotton Vehicles) .

(c) A truck-tractor operated in combination with a semitrailer and used to transport seed cotton or cotton may not be operated on a highway or road if the vehicle is higher than 14 feet 6 inches.

*Acts 2019, 86th Leg., R.S., Ch. 966 (S.B.688), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2019.*

wider than 10 feet and the highway has not been designated by the commission under Section 621.202 (Commission’s Authority to Set Maximum Width) ;
